name: Mock Test - 08
instruction: The Question Paper contains three sections. Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions. All questions carry equal marks. There is no negative marking.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is not a characteristic of a crystalline solid?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is not a characteristic of a crystalline solid?

options.text: A regular periodically repeated pattern of arrangement of constituent particles in the entire crystal.
options.html: A regular periodically repeated pattern of arrangement of constituent particles in the entire crystal.

options.text: Definite and characteristic heat of fusion.
options.html: Definite and characteristic heat of fusion.

questions.explanation: Isotropicity (equal values of physical properties) or anisotropicity (unequal values of symmetrical properties) is the property that deals with the distribution of inherent physical property( eg. - mechanical strength, refractive index) of any substance to the different molecular axes in accordance with the symmetry of the molecules of that substance. Crystalline solids are anisotropic in nature i.e. they show different values of physical properties like electrical resistance or refractive index when measuring along with different directions, unlike the amorphous solids. This is primarily because of their uneven arrangement of particles in different directions (x, y, and z axes). It depends upon the symmetry of the molecules which are arranged in a different way in all the planes (3-dimensional x, y, and z planes ) while amorphous solid are isotropic.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a Wurtz-Fitting reaction?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a Wurtz-Fitting reaction?
questions.explanation: A mixture of an alkyl halide and aryl halide gives an alkylarene when treated with sodium in dry ether and is called Wurtz-Fittig reaction. In above reaction ethyl bromide and bromobenzene react with sodium in dry ether to give ethyl benzene.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds will give racemic mixture on nucleophilic substitution by OH – ion? {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-Br{/tex} {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\overset{Br}{\mathop{\overset{|}{\mathop{\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,}}\,}}\,-CBr{/tex} {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-C{{H}_{2}}Br{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Which of the following compounds will give racemic mixture on nucleophilic substitution by OH– ion?

  1. {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-Br{/tex}
  2. {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\overset{Br}{\mathop{\overset{|}{\mathop{\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,}}\,}}\,-CBr{/tex}
  3. {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-C{{H}_{2}}Br{/tex}
questions.explanation: A mixture containing two enantiomers in equal proportions but with zero optical activity because the opposite optical rotations of the two enantiomers cancel out each other rotation is called a racemic mixture. Options (a) is a chiral carbon atom and it will undergo an S N 1 substitution mechanism to give a racemic mixture. Option (b) is not an asymmetric carbon. Option (c) contains a secondary carbon asymmetric atom, which has less reactivity towards an S N 1 substitution mechanism.

According to the rule, the hydrogen from HCl will get added to the carbon directly bonded to the most hydrogen atoms(addition reaction), and the –Cl will get bonded to the carbon directly bonded to the least hydrogens.

questions.explanation: When nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid at 5Íûå – position of sugar moiety, we get a nucleotide.

questions.question_text: The total number of tetrahedral voids in the face-centred unit cell is ________.
questions.question_html: The total number of tetrahedral voids in the face-centred unit cell is ________.
questions.explanation: We know that, if n be the number of atoms in a crystal, then the number of tetrahedral voids must be 2n. And for a face-centred unit cell - There are 8 Corner atoms i.e. (8 {tex}\times{/tex} 1/8) atoms per unit cell = 1atom and for face-centred unit cell atom number is 6 i.e.( 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 1/2) atoms per unit cell = 3 atom (because each atom at the face centre is used by to adjacent unit cells) Hence no. of tetrahedral voids for face-centred unit cell = 2(3+1) = 2(4) = 8.

questions.question_html: Solution of hydrogen in palladium is an example of
questions.explanation: Hydrogen (solute, gas) and solvent is palladium (solid).

questions.question_text: Which among the following forms basic oxide?
questions.question_html: Which among the following forms basic oxide?

questions.question_text: Grignard reagent should be prepared under anhydrous conditions because:
questions.question_html: Grignard reagent should be prepared under anhydrous conditions because:

options.text: it can react with any source of protons to give hydrocarbons
options.html: it can react with any source of protons to give hydrocarbons

options.text: the carbon – Mg bond is covalent but highly polar
options.html: the carbon – Mg bond is covalent but highly polar

options.text: the Mg – Halogen bond is essentially ionic
options.html: the Mg – Halogen bond is essentially ionic

questions.explanation: In the Grignard reagent, the carbon-magnesium bond is covalent but highly polar, with carbon pulling electrons from electropositive magnesium; the magnesium halogen bond is essentially ionic. Grignard reagents are highly reactive and react with any source of a proton to give hydrocarbons. Even water, alcohols, amines are sufficiently acidic to convert them to corresponding hydrocarbons. {tex}RMgX + H_2O \rightarrow RH + Mg(OH)X{/tex} It is therefore necessary to avoid even traces of moisture from a Grignard reagent.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of the following compound is {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex} is ________.
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of the following compound is {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation: If the oxygen is not attached to the end of the main alkane chain, then the whole shorter alkyl-plus-ether group is treated as a side-chain and prefixed with its bonding position on the main chain. Thus CH 3 OCH(CH 3 ) 2 is 2-methoxypropane.

questions.question_text: Protein is generally a polypeptide with _______ amino acid residues.
questions.question_html: Protein is generally a polypeptide with _______ amino acid residues.
questions.explanation: A polypeptide with more than hundred amino acid residues, having molecular mass higher than 10,000 u is called a protein. However, the distinction between a polypeptide and a protein is not very sharp. Polypeptides with fewer amino acids are likely to be called proteins if they ordinarily have a well defined conformation of a protein such as insulin which contains 51 amino acids.

questions.question_text: Schottky defect is observed in crystals when:
questions.question_html: Schottky defect is observed in crystals when:

options.text: some lattice sites are occupied by electrons.
options.html: some lattice sites are occupied by electrons.

options.text: equal number of cations and anions are missing from the lattice.
options.html: equal number of cations and anions are missing from the lattice.

options.text: some impurity is present in the lattice.
options.html: some impurity is present in the lattice.

options.text: some cations move from their lattice site to interstitial sites.
options.html: some cations move from their lattice site to interstitial sites.

questions.correct_option_text: equal number of cations and anions are missing from the lattice.
questions.explanation: The Schottky defect is basically a kind of vacancy defect with the tendency to maintain electrical neutrality, therefore the same no.s of cations are anions are missing from the crystal lattice Schottky defect also decrease the density of the substance.

questions.question_text: The concentration of a solution is {tex}0.3 \times {10^{ - 2}}{/tex} moles/litre. The osmotic pressure of the solution at 300K is
questions.question_html: The concentration of a solution is {tex}0.3 \times {10^{ - 2}}{/tex} moles/litre. The osmotic pressure of the solution at 300K is
questions.explanation: since {tex}\pi = cRT{/tex} R = 0.0821L atmK -1 mol -1 {tex}C = 0.3 \times {10^{ - 2}}{/tex} T = 300K on substituting the value we get, {tex}= 0.3 \times {10^{ - 2}} \times0.0821 \times300{/tex} = 0.0821atm

questions.question_text: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used in the preparation of caprolactam?
questions.explanation: NH 3 is used in the preparation of caprolactam. Caprolactam is an organic compound with the formula (CH 2 ) 5 C(O)NH. It is used to make Nylon 6 fibre and plastics.

questions.question_text: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.explanation: Liquid NH 3 is used as a refrigerant. evaporation of a liquid needs heat energy. When liquid ammonia vapourises, it absorbs large quantities of heat without changing its temperature.

questions.question_text: Phenol can be distinguished from ethanol by the reactions with _________.
questions.question_html: Phenol can be distinguished from ethanol by the reactions with _________.
questions.explanation: Phenol and ethanol can be easily distinguished by the ferric chloride test. When to a solution of phenol, a few drops of ferric chloride is added, a purple colouration is observed due to the formation of the following complex. 3C 6 H 5 OH + FeCl 3 {tex}\to {/tex} Fe(OC 6 H 5 ) 3 + 3HCl Ethanol, on the other hand, gives no such test, (Alcohols do not form colored complexes with iron ion) as the corresponding complex in case of ethanol is not formed.

questions.question_text: {tex}\mathop {{C_{12}}{H_{22}}{O_{11}}}\limits_{Maltose} \to \mathop {2{C_6}{H_{12}}{O_6}}\limits_{Glu\cos e} {/tex} In the above reaction, the enzyme that catalyses hydrolysis of maltose into glucose is
questions.question_html:
{tex}\mathop {{C_{12}}{H_{22}}{O_{11}}}\limits_{Maltose} \to \mathop {2{C_6}{H_{12}}{O_6}}\limits_{Glu\cos e} {/tex}
In the above reaction, the enzyme that catalyses hydrolysis of maltose into glucose is
questions.explanation: The enzyme which is responsible for catalysing the hydrolysis of maltose to glucose in the given reaction is named as maltase.

questions.question_text: Which law shows relation between pressure and solubility of a gas in solvent
questions.question_html: Which law shows relation between pressure and solubility of a gas in solvent
questions.explanation: Henry’s Law states that as the partial pressure of gas increases over liquid solubility of gas also increases.

questions.question_text: Sucrose (cane sugar) is a disaccharide. One molecule of sucrose on hydrolysis gives ________.
questions.question_html: Sucrose (cane sugar) is a disaccharide. One molecule of sucrose on hydrolysis gives ________.

options.text: 2 molecules of glucose + 1 molecule of fructose
options.html: 2 molecules of glucose + 1 molecule of fructose

options.text: 1 molecule of glucose + 1 molecule of fructose
options.html: 1 molecule of glucose + 1 molecule of fructose

questions.correct_option_text: 1 molecule of glucose + 1 molecule of fructose
questions.explanation: Sucrose (cane sugar) is a disaccharide(form by two monosaccharides). One molecule of sucrose on hydrolysis gives one molecule of glucose and one molecule of fructose. {tex}\underset{Cane\;sugar}{{\mathrm C}_{12}{\mathrm H}_{22}{\mathrm O}_{11}}\xrightarrow[\mathrm H^+]{{\mathrm H}_2\mathrm O}\underset{D(+)\;glu\cos e}{{\mathrm C}_6{\mathrm H}_{12}{\mathrm O}_6}+\underset{\mathrm D(-)\;\mathrm{furctose}}{{\mathrm C}_6{\mathrm H}_{12}{\mathrm O}_6}{/tex} Note: Sucrose is a dextrorotatory(D) sugar on hydrolysis produces a laevorotatory(L) mixture, so known as invert sugar. Sucrose is a non-reducing sugar while maltose and lactose are reducing sugar.

questions.question_text: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.question_html: Elements of which group are known as ore forming elements?
questions.explanation: A maximum number of ores contain group 16 elements (O, S, etc). They are known as "chalcogens" which was derived from the Greek word chalcos, meaning "ore formers," since they all are be found in copper ores.

questions.question_text: Which compound in the following pair reacts faster in S N 2 reaction with OH – ? CH 3 Br or CH 3 CH 3 Cl, (CH 3 ) 3 CCl
questions.question_html:
Which compound in the following pair reacts faster in SN2 reaction with OH–?

  1. CH3Br or CH3
  2. CH3Cl, (CH3)3CCl
questions.explanation: For the S N 2 reaction leaving group should be good and alkyl halide should be primary. So, CH 3 Br and CH 3 Cl are primary organic compounds. Hence the rate of S N 2 reaction will be more.

questions.question_text: The thermal stability of hydrides of group 16 elements decreases in the order:
questions.question_html: The thermal stability of hydrides of group 16 elements decreases in the order:

options.text: H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Te, H 2 Se
H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te
H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te,H 2 O
H 2 S, H 2 O H 2 Se, H 2 Te

questions.correct_option_text: H 2 O, H 2 S, H 2 Se, H 2 Te
questions.explanation: As we move down the group bond length increases and bond enthalpy for the dissociation of H-E bond decreases. Due to a decrease in enthalpy, the thermal stability of hydrides decreases down the group.

questions.question_html: London forces are present in
questions.explanation: London Forces are present in non polar molecular solids.Non polar Molecular Solids comprise of either atoms, for example, argon and helium or the molecules formed by non polar covalent bonds for example H 2 , Cl 2 and I 2 . In these solids, the atoms or molecules are held by weak dispersion forces or London forces.

questions.question_html: Which among the following does not exhibit a positive oxidation state?

questions.question_text: Aldehydes are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by the addition of hydrogen in the presence of catalysts to form ________.
questions.question_html: Aldehydes are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by the addition of hydrogen in the presence of catalysts to form ________.
questions.explanation: RCHO + H 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCH 2 OH Aldehydes and ketones are most readily reduced with hydride reagents. The reducing agents LiAlH 4 and NaBH 4 act as a source of 4 x H - (hydride ion) Overall 2 H atoms are added across the C=O to give H-C-O-H Hydride reacts with the carbonyl group, C=O, in aldehydes or ketones to give alcohols. The substituents on the carbonyl dictate the nature of the product alcohol. Reduction of methanal (formaldehyde) gives methanol. Reduction of other aldehydes gives primary alcohol. Reduction of ketones gives secondary alcohol.

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: Few colligative properties are: relative lowering of vapor pressure: depends only on the molar concentration of solute (mole fraction) and is independent of its nature. depression in freezing point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of the solution. elevation of the boiling point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of solute. osmotic pressure: it is proportional to the molar concentration of solute. A solution of glucose is prepared with 0.052 g at glucose in 80.2 g of water. (K f = 1.86 K kg mol -1 and K b = 5.4 K kg mol -1 Mole fraction of glucose in the given solution is
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

Few colligative properties are:

  1. relative lowering of vapor pressure: depends only on the molar concentration of solute (mole fraction) and is independent of its nature.
  2. depression in freezing point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of the solution.
  3. elevation of the boiling point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of solute.
  4. osmotic pressure: it is proportional to the molar concentration of solute.

A solution of glucose is prepared with 0.052 g at glucose in 80.2 g of water. (Kf = 1.86 K kg mol-1 and Kb = 5.4 K kg mol-1


Mole fraction of glucose in the given solution is
questions.explanation: Moles of glucose = {tex}\frac{0.052}{180}{/tex} = 0.00028 Moles of water = {tex}\frac{80.2}{18}{/tex} = 4.455 Mole fraction of glucose = {tex}\frac{0.00028}{4.45+0.00028}{/tex} = 6.28 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -5

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: Few colligative properties are: relative lowering of vapor pressure: depends only on the molar concentration of solute (mole fraction) and is independent of its nature. depression in freezing point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of the solution. elevation of the boiling point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of solute. osmotic pressure: it is proportional to the molar concentration of solute. A solution of glucose is prepared with 0.052 g at glucose in 80.2 g of water. (K f = 1.86 K kg mol -1 and K b = 5.4 K kg mol -1 If same amount of sucrose (C 12 H 22 O 11 ) is taken instead of glucose then elevation in boiling point will be higher depression in freezing point will be higher depression in freezing point will be lower both (a) and (b)
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

Few colligative properties are:

  1. relative lowering of vapor pressure: depends only on the molar concentration of solute (mole fraction) and is independent of its nature.
  2. depression in freezing point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of the solution.
  3. elevation of the boiling point: it is proportional to the molal concentration of solute.
  4. osmotic pressure: it is proportional to the molar concentration of solute.

A solution of glucose is prepared with 0.052 g at glucose in 80.2 g of water. (Kf = 1.86 K kg mol-1 and Kb = 5.4 K kg mol-1


If same amount of sucrose (C12H22O11) is taken instead of glucose then

  1. elevation in boiling point will be higher
  2. depression in freezing point will be higher
  3. depression in freezing point will be lower
  4. both (a) and (b)
questions.explanation: Depression in freezing point or elevation in boiling point is proportional to molarity which is proportional to number of moles. For same amount, higher the molar mass of solute lower will be number of moles. Hence, lower will be the colligative property.
